key_insight: WeldMamba predicts future molten-pool, keyhole, and wire regions from past images plus process/sensor signals, enabling lookahead weld-pool segmentation under control delay.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical perception layer for closed-loop laser wire-feed welding, where anticipating pool geometry can improve timing, stability, and actuator coordination.
method: Historical coaxial grayscale images are fused with welding parameters and wire-state electrical signals.
A physics-guided spatiotemporal state-space network models patch-level temporal dynamics and horizon-conditioned future features.
A motion-aware decoder with auxiliary geometric and temporal losses refines keyhole, wire, and molten-pool masks.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieves 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead, with ablations showing gains from temporal history, patch-level state-space modeling, and keyhole motion awareness.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated on a relatively small dataset, so robustness to different welding setups, materials, and sensing conditions still needs validation.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
